q: They (bring) some boxes tomorrow.
a: They are going to bring some boxes tomorrow.

q: The headmaster (speak) to us tomorrow.
a: The headmaster is going to speak to us tomorrow.

q: I (attend) the meeting this Monday.
a: I am going to attend the meeting this Monday.

q: We (play) golf tomorrow afternoon.
a: We are going to play golf tomorrow afternoon.

q: He (buy) some oranges tomorrow.
a: He is going to buy some oranges tomorrow.

q: The sky is dark. It (rain).
a: The sky is dark. It is going to rain.

q: They (work) on Saturday.
a: They are going to work on Saturday.

q: The plane (leave) in an hour.
a: The plane is going to leave in an hour.

q: Be careful! The branch (break).
a: Be careful! The branch is going to break.

q: I (see) the headmaster tomorrow.
a: I am going to see the headmaster tomorrow.

q: She (dance) again this evening.
a: She is going to dance again this evening.

q: He (show) me the book.
a: He is going to show me the book.

q: My uncle (leave) here tomorrow.
a: My uncle is going to leave here tomorrow.

q: He (finish) the work next week.
a: He is going to finish the work next week.

q: My brother (put) on that shirt tomorrow morning.
a: My brother is going to put on that shirt tomorrow morning.

q: They (play) the game next Friday.
a: They are going to play the game next Friday.

q: Helen (see) her uncle next week.
a: Helen is going to see her uncle next week.

q: We (paint) the house this weekend.
a: We are going to paint the house this weekend.

q: I (tell) you the answer.
a: I am going to tell you the answer.

q: We (cut) down that tree this evening.
a: We are going to cut down that tree this evening.

q: She (write) him a letter tonight.
a: She is going to write him a letter tonight.

q: My father (take) me to the circus tomorrow.
a: My father is going to take me to the circus tomorrow.

q: He (swim) in the pool tomorrow.
a: He is going to swim in the pool tomorrow.

q: They (sleep) in this room tonight.
a: They are going to sleep in this room tonight.

q: She (make) a new dress this afternoon.
a: She is going to make a new dress this afternoon.

q: Hold on, I (get) you a glass of water.
a: Hold on, I will get you a glass of water.

q: I promise I (not forget) to call you.
a: I promise I will not forget to call you.

q: That glass is on the edge of the table! It (fall)!
a: That glass is on the edge of the table! It is going to fall!

q: (What / you / order)? Have you looked at the menu yet?
a: What are you going to order? Have you looked at the menu yet?

q: We (celebrate) our anniversary in Bagan — we've booked everything.
a: We are going to celebrate our anniversary in Bagan — we've booked everything.

q: They (move) to a new apartment next week — they've already packed.
a: They are going to move to a new apartment next week — they've already packed.

q: Look at those black clouds: It (rain) soon.
a: Look at those black clouds: It is going to rain soon.

q: He (probably buy) a new car next month. That's what he told me.
a: He is probably going to buy a new car next month. That's what he told me.

q: I (not join) the game. I've already told the team I need to study.
a: I am not going to join the game. I've already told the team I need to study.

q: I (meet) him at the airport — I just decided.
a: I will meet him at the airport — I just decided.

q: (Who / answer) the phone? It's been ringing for a while.
a: Who will answer the phone? It's been ringing for a while.

q: I (study) medicine after high school. That's my long-term plan.
a: I am going to study medicine after high school. That's my long-term plan.

q: She (not finish) the task on time — she hasn't even started.
a: She is not going to finish the task on time — she hasn't even started.

q: I think our teacher (be) happy with our project.
a: I think our teacher will be happy with our project.

q: I can't carry this bag. Okay, I (help) you.
a: I can't carry this bag. Okay, I will help you.

q: Don't worry about the food. I (cook) something for everyone.
a: Don't worry about the food. I will cook something for everyone.

q: We (not stay) long. We only planned a short visit.
a: We are not going to stay long. We only planned a short visit.

q: They (not attend) the wedding. They already said no.
a: They are not going to attend the wedding. They already said no.

q: (When / you / visit) your grandparents? Have you set a date?
a: When are you going to visit your grandparents? Have you set a date?

q: I (be) careful — I promise!
a: I will be careful — I promise!
